Glossary

0–9

0-Euro Cockpit: A self-built, free digital command center for one's own company, which manages without expensive licenses and merely requires a web browser and text editor.

A

API (Application Programming Interface): A programming interface that functions like a digital "waiter". It mediates data and requests between the dashboard and external services (such as an AI or database) without the webpage having to be reloaded.

B

BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation): A global industry standard for the formal and legally compliant modeling of business processes. Works visually with so-called swimlanes (responsibilities) and gateways (branches).

C

C4 Model: A pragmatic IT architecture model (Context, Container, Components, Code) that visualizes IT landscapes and dependencies like a zoomable map – from the broad system context deep into the code.

CDN (Content Delivery Network): A global network for delivering content. Used to integrate external code libraries (e.g., for diagrams) directly into your cockpit's HTML file without having to download them to your own hard drive first.

Chart.js: A popular and beginner-friendly JavaScript library for visualizing numbers in HTML files as appealing pie or bar charts.

Cockpit (vs. Dashboard): While a pure dashboard merely displays data, a cockpit is designed to actively control processes (command center). The dashboard is thus a functional component of the cockpit.
completed.24:
Cockpit (vs. Dashboard): While a pure dashboard merely displays data, a cockpit is designed to actively steer processes (command center). The dashboard is thus a functional building block of the cockpit.

CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing): A security mechanism in the browser. It can trigger error messages if your local HTML file tries to grab data directly from a foreign API (like a cloud AI provider).

CRM (Customer Relationship Management): Software for managing customer relationships – e.g., for sales, customer service, and contact maintenance. Centrally stores who your customers are, what they have bought, and what communication has taken place.

CSV (Comma-Separated Values): A simple, text-based file format for storing tabular data. Ideal for transferring export data from banks or CRM systems to an AI.

D

Diagram-as-Code (DaC): A method where flowcharts or IT architectures are not painstakingly drawn by mouse, but described in simple lines of text. The software generates and updates the final image from this fully automatically.

Doc Rot (Documentation Decay): The phenomenon where manually created graphics and processes become outdated extremely quickly because the effort to change them in programs like PowerPoint is too high in everyday life.

DOM (Document Object Model): The internal structure of a webpage as the browser "sees" it. Via DOM manipulation, JavaScript can change individual elements (e.g., a KPI tile) live without having to reload the entire page.

Dropzone: A graphical area in your cockpit where you can easily drag and drop files (like Excel or CSV) with the mouse to update data in the background.

G

GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation): European regulatory framework for data processing. Legally regulates which personal data is allowed to leave your local system and which must absolutely be anonymized before a cloud upload.

E

EAM (Enterprise Architecture Management): The professional and structured management of the entire enterprise architecture – including business processes, data structures, application landscape, and technical infrastructure.

ERP System (Enterprise Resource Planning): Complex standard software for the holistic planning of company resources (personnel, finances, materials). Often associated with high licensing and implementation costs.

F

Fetch API: A built-in JavaScript function of your web browser that communicates silently in the background with APIs to fetch live data or send AI commands.

H

HTML (Hypertext Markup Language): The foundational text markup language that defines the framework and structure of your cockpit in the web browser.

html2canvas: A library that makes it possible via code to generate high-resolution screenshots (PNGs) directly from the elements of your HTML cockpit.

J

JSFiddle: A free online sandbox for web developers. Perfect for beginners to safely test generated HTML and JavaScript code directly in the browser and adapt it in real-time.

JSON (JavaScript Object Notation): A clean, structured text format in which APIs and AI models exchange their data packets so that your script can process them flawlessly and display them as a diagram.

K

KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A metric that measures success or progress in a specific area – e.g., revenue, vacancy rate, or open tickets. In the cockpit, KPIs are displayed as tiles on the dashboard.

L

LLM (Large Language Model): Large language model. It forms the technological core of modern generative AI systems (like Google Gemini, ChatGPT, or locally with Ollama) that understands and generates human language and programming code.

M

Mermaid.js: An open-source tool that transforms simple text code directly in the web browser into dynamic flowcharts, pie charts, or process workflows.

Meta-Prompting: The technique of asking an AI to write the perfect, highly professional "master prompt" for another task (e.g., programming a complex dashboard).

N

n8n: A visual automation tool that moves data between your emails, AI models, and spreadsheets in the background like a digital assembly line worker.

No-Coder / Low-Coder: Users who have not studied classic programming languages but build complex software solutions with the help of AI, visual editors, and clear instructions.

Nodes & Edges: The basic building blocks of a knowledge graph. Nodes are the actors (people, companies, tools), edges are the connecting lines that represent the relationship between them.

O

Ollama: A program to run powerful AI models completely locally and offline on your own computer. Guarantees 100% data security when processing highly sensitive corporate documents.

OpenClaw: A framework for autonomous AI agents that not only wait for commands but also independently coordinate and process defined workflows (such as classifying tickets).

P

PapaParse: An extremely fast and error-resistant JavaScript library that reads CSV files directly in the browser and makes them usable for your cockpit. 

PlantUML: A text-based "Diagram-as-Code" language that is particularly strong at displaying complex IT architectures and C4 models in a structured way.

Prompt: The command or precise work instruction that you type into the chat for the AI in order to obtain the desired result (analysis, code, diagram).

S

Sankey Diagram: A specific type of chart where flows of money or data are depicted as bands of varying widths. The wider the band, the larger the amount – ideal for seeing at a glance where the money is flowing.

SheetJS: A powerful open-source library that allows your browser to read and process Excel files (.xlsx) locally without uploading them to a server.

Single Source of Truth (SSOT): A system or location (in this case, the cockpit) where all valid data, processes, and the entire company knowledge converge centrally, error-free, and bindingly.

Swimlanes: A graphical element in the BPMN model ("swimming lanes"). They visually divide a process according to the responsible people, departments, or systems.

V

Vis.js: A powerful JavaScript library for displaying interactive networks and knowledge graphs directly in the browser, into which you can zoom and move nodes via drag-and-drop.

W

Knowledge Graph: An interactive, visual spider web. It makes the invisible threads of your company (Who communicates with whom? Which tool has which dependencies?) tangible and uncovers hidden risks.

XML (Extensible Markup Language): A markup language that structures data hierarchically. Used in this book primarily as an export format for diagram tools (like draw.io).

Cheat Sheet: The 0-Euro Cockpit

The universal master prompt:

I want to [insert goal, e.g., build a digital company cockpit]. Write me the professional prompt that I can give to a coding AI so it implements this perfectly for me.

Data Privacy Decision Tree

Question: Would it be bad if this data were in the newspaper tomorrow?

YES (Bank details, contracts, HR): Solution: Use local AI.

Tool: Start Ollama (ollama.com) (ollama run qwen3.5:4b).

Option 1: Anonymize with Ollama, then continue working with cloud models.

Option 2: Work exclusively locally (powerful hardware required).

NO (Marketing, general processes): Solution: Use cloud AI.

Tools: ChatGPT (chatgpt.com) or Gemini (gemini.google.com).

Important: It is mandatory to deactivate AI training in the settings!

Diagram-as-Code: Which tool for what?

Instead of drawing, you describe the structure as text. The AI generates the code, the browser paints the picture.

Tip for design adjustments: Import generated code into diagrams.net (app.diagrams.net) and refine it via drag-and-drop.

The Cockpit Foundation 

Your cockpit is a single, local HTML file. You simply edit it in a text editor.

Testing without saving: Use JSFiddle (jsfiddle.net) to test code changes in real-time and with absolutely zero risk (Ctrl+Z saves everything).

Local Updates (Dropzone): Use libraries like SheetJS and PapaParse via CDN to read CSV and Excel files locally in the browser without data leaving your system.

API Interfaces (The "Waiter"): Use the JavaScript fetch() function to pull data from an AI into your cockpit.

JSON constraint: Always use the following addition in the prompt: "Answer EXCLUSIVELY in JSON format, no introduction."

Hosting & Sharing : 

Variant A (Secure Vault): Upload your cockpit to a private GitHub repository. Share it only via the "Collaborators" function with your team. Colleagues download the file and open it locally.

Variant B (Live link with Cloudflare): For a real web link, use Cloudflare Pages in combination with Cloudflare Zero Trust.

Attention GitHub Pages: Only activate GitHub Pages if your cockpit contains absolutely no sensitive data (e.g., only public marketing). With free GitHub accounts, Pages websites are often publicly viewable, even if the repository is private!

Quick Troubleshooting (The 4 Rescuers)

Error messages in the diagram: Don't debug it yourself! Copy the error message, paste it into the AI and say: "There was an error here, correct it."

Browser shows code instead of dashboard: Your file is secretly named cockpit.html.txt. Show file extensions in the operating system and delete the .txt!

CORS Blockage (Console shows error): Your browser is blocking the cloud API for security reasons. Either use a local AI (Ollama) or OpenRouter (openrouter.ai), which offer CORS-friendly endpoints.

Layout breaks in Mermaid: The AI choked on special characters. Simply put text passages in the code in double quotes "...".
